1. To calculate the area enclosed by the polygon formed by the landmarks A(1, 3), B(4, 6), C(7, 2), and D(3, -1), we use the **Shoelace Theorem**. The formula is:

$$
\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \left| \sum_{i=1}^{n} x_i y_{i+1} - \sum_{i=1}^{n} y_i x_{i+1} \right|
$$

Where the coordinates are listed in order, and the last point connects back to the first. Plugging in the coordinates:

$$
\begin{align*}
\text{Sum}_1 &= (1 \cdot 6) + (4 \cdot 2) + (7 \cdot -1) + (3 \cdot 3) = 6 + 8 - 7 + 9 = 16 \\
\text{Sum}_2 &= (3 \cdot 4) + (6 \cdot 7) + (2 \cdot 3) + (-1 \cdot 1) = 12 + 42 + 6 - 1 = 60 \\
\text{Area} &= \frac{1}{2} |16 - 60| = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 44 = 22
\end{align*}
$$

So, the area enclosed by the polygon is **22 square units**.

---

$$
\text{Distance} = \sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2}
